free_text: Measurement and evaluation of integral data in the
Cf-252 neutron field
Same as 82ANTWER except for 59Co(n,a)56Mn,
58Ni(n,2n)57Ni, 58Ni(n,x)57Co, 60Ni(n,p)60Co
Cf-252 spontaneous fission neutron source,
encapsulated in a double zircaloy cylinder with
outer dimensions of 10 mm in diameter and 10 mm
in height.
The wall thickness of the cylinder was 1.5 mm.
Spectrum perturbations due to the incapsulation were
of the order of 1.%, somewhat dependent on the
neutron energy.
The neutron source strength was approximately
2.0x10**8 n/sec on July 1,1981.
58Ni(n,2n)57Ni, 63Cu(n,g)64Cu and 63Cu(n,2n)62Cu were
measured with another neutron source of the same size
but containing 2 mg of recently-produced californium.
In these cases the neutron source strength was
4.7x10**9 n/sec on April 1,1982.
Cf-252 neutron spectrum
High purity metallic foils with the exception
of the 19F(n,2n) reaction where teflon was used.
Irradiations performed in a low scattering
arrangement.
The irradiations were performed using the sandwich
method, i.e. each sample was placed between two
foils acting as neutron flux density monitors.
The activity of the short lived 18F, 27Mg and
62Cu was followed for seven half-lives to identify
the reactions uniquely and to correct for other
contributions.
Large volume Ge(Li) detector of a nominal
efficiency of 28%.
Atomic mass, isotopic abundance, decay data given
no details given.
Evaluated 23 252Cf PFNS averaged cross sections
given with their covariances
Approved by Wolf Mannhart (2014-02-14)
G.C.
A.P.T. Laboratory code corrected.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
corrected according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.
On. REFERENCE: 82GAITHERS added. Subents
015-017 were added. EN-DUMMY -> KT-DUMMY in Subents
002- 013 after discussion with author(Dr.W.Mannhart).
